blocks.content: That night. My room. Phone on the desk, homework half-done, the house quiet the way it gets after dinner when everyone's in their corners.
I thought about the week in order. The way you replay things sometimes when they're actually worth replaying.
I thought about Daniel looking away first. I thought about Crystal at the steps asking what I was afraid of. I thought about Ms. Barrett's note. About the route that finally clicked on Wednesday.
I thought about Mr. Cole's car, the smell of it, the way he drove — steady, not like someone who'd never been scared, but like someone who'd decided what to do with it.
I opened my desk drawer and found an empty notebook. Blue cover, college ruled. I'd bought it at the beginning of the school year for history class and never used it.
I flipped to the first page.
I wrote: The messy version is closer to true.
I don't know who that was for. Maybe just for me. A way of marking what had happened this week without turning it into something bigger than it was.
I left the rest of the page blank. For whatever came next.
